summary: Close the independent FR-1.4 performance and freshness audit with hard aggregate traversal budgets, path-and-source parse caching, a lazy open-buffer overlay, textual include ordering, and live completion invalidation.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.decision: Bound one completion traversal to 64 parsed files, 50,000 code units per file, 100,000 aggregate code units, 512 followed references, and 1,024 collected symbols.
decisions.rationale: The earlier per-file-only limit permitted 512 million code units plus unbounded missing-reference and symbol work on the renderer thread. Small explicit aggregate budgets preserve responsive editing and fail by omitting supplemental project suggestions rather than blocking source entry.

decisions.topic: Completion parsing and source lookup reuse
decisions.decision: Cache structurally parsed project events by normalized path and exact source, with the same aggregate memory ceiling, and replace per-edit full-project Map copies with a stable lazy snapshot lookup plus open-buffer overlays.
decisions.rationale: Referenced files normally remain unchanged while the active document is edited. Reusing their parse results and avoiding O(all project files) copies removes project-size work from repeated completion and keystroke render paths without making filesystem reads.

decisions.decision: Traverse referenced files depth first in textual include order so later declarations win, and close an active CodeMirror completion result whenever non-active project dependency context changes.
decisions.rationale: Breadth-first traversal allowed a nested earlier include to overwrite a later direct declaration, while a live popup could retain suggestions from replaced dependency source. Both behaviors contradicted the effective source order or current project state.

testEvidence.criterion: All independently reported completion correctness, cache, bound, hot-path, and popup findings reproduce before implementation
testEvidence.test: Focused completion, mounted CodeEditor, and project-context hook tests require textual duplicate precedence, one parse for unchanged source, aggregate and reference cutoff, popup invalidation, and stable lookup identity across active edits.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 3 failed; Tests 6 failed and 23 passed. Breadth-first order returned x.scad instead of b.scad; unchanged source parsed twice; both unbounded graphs reached late symbols; the popup remained active; and active edits rebuilt the context object.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 5 passed; Tests 50 passed. Every reported semantic, cache, aggregate-bound, lazy-lookup, and popup-freshness assertion passed together.

testEvidence.criterion: An adversarial reference fanout cannot monopolize the renderer thread
testEvidence.test: A 60,000-reference, 1.6-million-code-unit injected file is timed from completion request through result and must be rejected before parsing in under 100 ms.

red.command: Independent read-only audit reproduction against 768f855 using the production completion source
red.outcome: PERFORMANCE RED: the permitted adversarial file synchronously blocked for 415.6 ms; a 25.6-million-code-unit 256-file graph took 244.0 ms; and a 100,000-entry source-map rebuild took 29.4 ms per edit.

green.outcome: GREEN: the production completion request passed in 11 ms in the focused test, below the explicit 100 ms regression ceiling, because the oversized source was rejected before parsing.

testEvidence.criterion: Static contracts and source policy remain valid
testEvidence.test: Typecheck the lookup/context/cache contracts and lint the complete repository under the UI-size and platform-boundary policies.

red.outcome: STATIC RED DURING REFACTOR: TypeScript rejected the initial lookup interface mismatch and Biome rejected import ordering plus imprecise hook dependencies before the final contract was accepted.

green.outcome: GREEN: TypeScript exited 0; Biome checked 292 files with no fixes; source policy passed with every UI file within 400 lines and platform boundaries intact.

questions: The safety budgets limit only supplemental cross-file suggestions; current-file completion, editing, saving, rendering, and project files remain unchanged and available.
Q-0014 remains a historical scheduling question only; M2's C6-backed completion behavior is implemented.
No owner similarity gate, comparison repository, workflow content, or prohibited source was opened or executed.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
